content.full_text:
California Museum of Science and Industry

Come Touch
Tomorrow

February 16, 1990

Mr. John Larson
Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California  95804

Dear John:

Thanks for your offer of assistance with settling the
question of reporting for legislators and other officials
who receive parking passes for the Courtesy Parking Lot at
the California Museum of Science and Industry.

The passes are issued each August for the following year and
include museum staff, donors, corporate sponsors of
exhibits, volunteers and public officials.

We have received some inquiries from legislators with regard
to what amount they should include in their financial
reports for receipt of the passes.  The information they
have received has varied as there are numerous ways of
calculating the value.  It could be:

     1.  $1 per day - as that is the normal amount collected
at the gate.

     2.  Nothing - if the pass is never used!

     3.  $550.00 - the amount a Foundation member (donor)
must contribute in order to receive a pass.

     4.  $25,000 - the amount a corporation may have
contributed for exhibit upkeep or maintenance.

A copy of last year's policy is enclosed for your perusal.

If you could get a letter or opinion to us that we could
include in the mailing with the passes, that would certainly
be helpful.

It's always a pleasure to do business with you!

Sincerely,

Suzanne L. Glad
Deputy Director for External Affairs

700 State Drive  Los Angeles  CA 90037  213•744•7400

FPPC

FEB 20  9 21 AH '90

George Deukmejian
Governor

Board of Directors

Fred L. Riedman
President

James Dickason
Vice President

Michael D. Bagdasarian

Jesse A. Brewer

Neil Campbell

Senator William Campbell

N. Matthew Grossman

Alexander Haagen

Rodney W. Wood

Jeffrey N. Rudolph
Executive Director

MEMORANDUM TO: Fred Riedman, Jeff Rudolph, Mike Bagdasarian

FROM: Sue Glad, Deputy Director for External Affairs

RE: 1989-90 Parking Pass Distribution

DATE: August 1, 1989

Parking passes will be mailed today according to the following
policy which we discussed in May:

CATEGORY                                          # OF PASSES

CMSI Board of Directors                                   10
CMF Board of Trustees                                      1
Advisory Board members                                     1
CMSI Docents                                              1
MOSAIC members                                            1
The Muses Executive Board                                 1
California Wine Patrons Executive Board                   1
Economics and Finance Affiliates Executive Board          1
Aerospace Affiliates Executive Board                      1
Aerospace Historical Committee                            1
CMF Member donors
  $500.00                                                 1
  $2,500.00                                               2
  $5,000.00                                               3
CMSI/CMF/CAAM staff                                       1
Media                                                     1
State Legislators (Senate/Assembly)                       1
County Officials (Supervisors, others)                    1
City Officials (Mayor, City Council, Coliseum
  Representatives)                                        1
State Officials                                     1 (Gov-2)
Federal Officials (Congressmen, Senators, if
  requested)                                              1
Exhibit Sponsors, contributors, special support
  ($25,000 for 5 years, minimum)                        3-5

Duplications are eliminated - i.e CMF Board member, also on
Support Group Executive Board and a $500.00 donor gets one pass
unless contribution is $2,500.00 or more. However, a $500.00
donor whose company also contributes as an exhibit sponsor may
receive additional passes for his company, depending on the
amount of the annual sponsorship.

(State, County, City, Federal officials and exhibit sponsors,
contributors, special support individuals were reviewed by the
Administration and Finance Committee)
